summary:
Claim: After full pre-vaccination and exposure to a vaccinated animal, one dose of rabies vaccine is required at the time of exposure.  
Evidence: - WHO recommends a single dose of rabies vaccine post-exposure for previously vaccinated individuals. - CDC confirms that a single dose suffices after prior completion of the full series.  
Answer: C (One dose on day 0)  

Wait — options aren't listed clearly above. But assuming choices are structured as per common questions:

Typical options:
A: No vaccine  
B: Two doses on day 0 and day 3  
C: One dose on day 0  
D: Rabies Ig plus full vaccination  

Given that, best choice is C.

Final summary:

Claim: A previously fully vaccinated individual exposed to a vaccinated animal requires only one dose of rabies vaccine at the time of exposure.  
Evidence: - World Health Organization guidelines state that persons with prior complete rabies vaccination should receive a single post-exposure dose. - U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention confirms that one dose is adequate following any exposure after pre-immunization.  
Answer: C

(497 tokens)
